studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Primary endpoints are assessed directly after the 12-week intervention phase.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention group (app-guided exercise intervention) and Control group (no intervention), randomized 1:1.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention arm: 12-week home exercise program guided by a digital app, including progressive video-supported exercises to improve mobility, flexibility, strength, and balance, with movements controlled and documented by accelerometers for biofeedback. Control arm: No study intervention.
studies.collected_data: Knee Osteoarthritis Outcome Score (KOOS) Subscale Pain, KOOS Subscale Activities of daily living, health-related quality of life (VR12), fear of movement, other KOOS subscales, Patient's Global Assessment, leg strength and strength endurance (30-Second Chair Test), perceived exertion during exercising, exercise adherence, safety aspects (pain, unexpected events, side effects), usability, and patient satisfaction.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: 12-week intervention with 3 sessions per week; primary outcomes assessed at 3 months.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group received a 12-week self-directed app-based and sensor-assisted exercise program (re.flex) with 3 sessions per week in addition to usual care. Control group received usual care only.
studies.collected_data: OA-specific knee pain and physical function (using the subscales pain and activities in daily living of the Knee Osteoarthritis Outcome Score, 0-100) at 3 months; adherence; safety; adverse events.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Pain reduction was significantly larger in re.flex than in usual care, with an adjusted mean difference between study groups of 4.8 (95% CI 0.7-8.9; P=.02; Cohen d=0.35) points. Improvement in physical function was not statistically significant (beta coefficient [β]=3.9 points, 95% CI 0.0-7.9, P=.049). A total of 12 adverse events were linked to re.flex, none of which were serious. Participants adhered to 77% (2705/3528) of all scheduled exercise sessions.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: 12-week home exercise program with 3 sessions per week. Outcomes were assessed at baseline and after 12 weeks.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention group (IG): n=30; Control group (CG): n=31
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group (IG) had 2 subgroups: app-based training (IG A) and app-based training with a supportive knee brace (IG AB). The intervention was a 12-week home exercise program (3 sessions/week) instructed via app and monitored using 2 accelerometers. The control group (CG) received no study intervention but used usual care.
studies.collected_data: Osteoarthritis-specific pain (Knee Injury and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score [KOOS]), all other KOOS subscales, general health-related quality of life (Veterans RAND 12-item Health Survey), psychological measures (e.g., exercise self-efficacy), performance measures (strength and postural control), adherence, and safety.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Statistically significant effects in favor of the IG for pain reduction (P<.001; ES=0.76), improvements in physical function (P<.001; ES=0.64), symptoms (P=.01; ES=0.53), sport and recreation activities (P=.02; ES=0.47), knee-related quality of life (P<.001; ES=0.76), and the physical component of general health-related quality of life (P<.001; ES=0.74). Mean differences ranged from 6.0 to 13.2 points. No effects were found for psychological and performance measures. Participants adhered to 92.5% (899/972) of all scheduled exercise sessions.

audit.study_title: Randomized controlled trial to evaluate a 12-week app-guided exercise intervention in patients with knee osteoarthritis (re.flex)
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title: Effectiveness of the Self-Directed mHealth Exercise Intervention re.flex in Patients With Knee Osteoarthritis: Randomized Controlled Trial.
Efficacy of the mHealth-Based Exercise Intervention re.flex for Patients With Knee Osteoarthritis: Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial.
